title: solanum villosum subsp. ochroleucum
intro: Solanum villosum subsp. ochroleucum is a plant species within the Solanum genus, characterized by its hairy stems and yellowish-green leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The plant has been reported to provide several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Solanum villosum subsp. ochroleucum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
